title: How Withholding Taxes Affect Cross-Border Investment in Europe
summary: Because withholding taxes can create double taxation and administrative friction even where foreign tax credits are available, the European Commission’s 2026 Tax Omnibus proposal to eliminate them on dividend, interest, and royalty payments between EU companies regardless of holding percentage would foster stronger cross-border savings and investment.
ai_summary: Withholding taxes are identified as impediments to cross-border investment in Europe, causing double taxation and administrative burdens. The European Commission’s 2026 Tax Omnibus proposal aims to mitigate these effects by eliminating withholding taxes on dividend, interest, and royalty payments between EU companies to foster stronger regional investment.

title: Taxing the Matchmakers: How Digital Services Taxes Cascade Through Online Commerce
summary: A destination-based value-added tax (VAT) or goods and services tax (GST) is a better instrument for taxing digital consumption by avoiding cascading. Recent experience shows that governments can tax digital consumption with a better structure than DSTs.
ai_summary: This analysis critiques Digital Services Taxes (DSTs), arguing that they can lead to cascading effects through online commerce. It proposes that destination-based VAT or GST systems offer a more effective and less distortive instrument for taxing digital consumption by avoiding such cascading.
